title: Fault Lines 2024-2026: Iran-Israel Regional Escalation
learning_objectives: Explore escalation management after direct state-on-state strikes between Israel and Iran.
Assess tradeoffs between military success, coalition legitimacy, humanitarian pressure, and global economic stability.
Examine how proxy degradation, nuclear risk, domestic instability, and maritime chokepoints interact in a regional crisis.
Evaluate roles of PRC and Russia as diplomatic, energy, and arms-support actors rather than passive observers.

players.label: United States of America
players.victory_condition: Score by protecting Israel and US bases, degrading Iran nuclear/missile capability, maintaining coalition cohesion, reopening maritime trade, and avoiding uncontrolled escalation or severe legal backlash.
players.notes: Central Blue actor with powerful military options but high legal, humanitarian, and global-market exposure.

players.label: NATO and European Union
players.victory_condition: Score by sustaining transatlantic unity, defending trade and bases, limiting civilian harm, preserving legal legitimacy, and securing a ceasefire that reopens Hormuz.
players.notes: Diplomatic and economic weight; limited direct offensive role.

players.label: Israel
players.victory_condition: Score by disrupting IRGC and proxy networks, preventing nuclear breakout, protecting Israeli population centers, and keeping enough US/NATO support to sustain operations.
players.notes: Highly capable but escalation-prone; offensive success often generates legitimacy costs.

players.label: Iran
players.victory_condition: Score by preserving regime continuity, sustaining enough missile/proxy/asymmetric capability to impose costs, fracturing Blue cohesion, gaining sanctions relief or reparations, and preventing decisive Israeli/US freedom of action.
players.notes: Primary Red actor; can impose global costs but risks collapse through military defeat, economic crisis, and domestic revolt.

players.label: China
players.victory_condition: Score by constraining US freedom of action, preserving energy access, brokering favorable ceasefire terms, expanding influence in Iran and the Gulf, and avoiding direct war.
players.notes: Uses diplomacy, energy leverage, sanctions evasion, and narratives; rarely destroys forces directly.

players.label: Russia
players.victory_condition: Score by exploiting energy-market disruption, weakening NATO unity, preserving influence in Syria/Iran, gaining sanctions relief, and avoiding overextension.
players.notes: Resource-constrained but can obstruct diplomacy, provide limited technical support, and exploit energy crisis.

locations.label: UK/NATO Support Bases: Cyprus and Bahrain

bases.notes: Israeli central command and air bases.
US regional air command node exposed to Iranian retaliation.
European support, air defense, and maritime coordination bases.
Asymmetric naval forces and Hormuz control.

proxy_forces.label: Hezbollah remnants in Lebanon
proxy_forces.scope: Rocket, ATGM, and local ground attacks; degraded after decapitation.

proxy_forces.label: Houthi Maritime Disruption Cell
proxy_forces.scope: Information-linked maritime disruption and drone threat.

victory_conditions.value: Win if Iranian nuclear risk is reduced by 3+, Hormuz is open or stabilized, Israel survives major missile attacks, and Blue coalition cohesion remains at 3+.
Win if humanitarian/legal pressure is contained, energy stability is restored to 3+, and ceasefire framework exists by game end.
Win if Hezbollah/proxy capability is reduced by 3+, Iranian missile/nuclear threat is reduced, and Israeli domestic security remains credible.
Win if regime continuity survives, at least one coercive lever remains at 3+, and Blue coalition cohesion is degraded or reparations/sanctions relief are on the table.
Win if PRC secures energy exemptions or stabilizes flows, gains ceasefire leverage, and US unilateral freedom of action is constrained.
Win if Russia gains energy leverage/sanctions benefit, NATO unity is reduced, and Russian influence in Syria/Iran is not eliminated.

value.title: Integrated Air Defense Coalition
value.description: Coordinate US, UK, French, Jordanian, and Israeli air-defense networks against Iranian missile and drone salvos.

play_constraints.frequency: Once per game; best on Turn 1 or in response to Iranian missile attack.

response.cost_rule: Iran may trigger a retaliation narrative or expend additional missile inventory.

value.notes: Strong defensive value but consumes interceptors and exposes partners.

value.title: B-2 Deep Strike on Nuclear Infrastructure
value.description: Commit US stealth bombers and bunker-buster munitions against Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan after nuclear breakout indicators.

play_constraints.frequency: Once per game; Turn 3 or later.

prerequisites.description: Nuclear Risk scenario flag is 4+ or White Cell judges imminent breakout.

response.cost_rule: Iran may launch ballistic retaliation; PRC/RU may impose diplomatic costs.

modifiers.description: If Iran Air Defense not previously degraded, apply penalty.

outcome_map.label: Facilities shattered
outcome_map.narrative: Deep buried nuclear sites suffer decisive damage.

outcome_map.label: Damage uncertain
outcome_map.narrative: Strike triggers backlash with only partial military clarity.

value.notes: Powerful but escalatory and legally controversial.

value.title: Operation Epic Fury
value.description: A regime-change-oriented air campaign aimed at leadership compounds, missile forces, and naval assets.

play_constraints.frequency: Once per game; Turn 4 only unless White Cell permits.

prerequisites.description: Iranian protest or legitimacy crisis has occurred.

response.cost_rule: Iran may retaliate against bases; NATO/EU may distance itself.

value.notes: May not collapse regime; creates major backlash.

value.title: Counter-Blockade of Iranian Ports
value.description: US naval forces cut off Iranian port revenue while attempting to reopen Hormuz.

play_constraints.frequency: Once; Turn 5 or after Hormuz Closure.

prerequisites.description: Hormuz Closure card or equivalent flag is active.

response.cost_rule: Iran may mine or missile shipping lanes; PRC may demand exemptions.

outcome_map.label: Sea lanes partially reopen
outcome_map.narrative: Iranian revenue is squeezed and shipping cautiously resumes.

value.notes: Can stabilize or worsen global markets.

value.title: Ceasefire Ultimatum and Backchannel
value.description: Offer a coercive off-ramp: halt strikes for Hormuz reopening and missile stand-down, backed by threat of renewed strikes.

response.cost_rule: Iran may demand reparations; Israel may resist constraints.

value.notes: Requires other players to accept terms for full effect.

value.title: European Air and Maritime Shield
value.description: Deploy air-defense and naval assets from Cyprus, Bahrain, and Mediterranean stations to protect bases and commerce.

play_constraints.frequency: Once per turn if resources allow.

response.cost_rule: Iran may target support bases; Russia may exploit NATO strain.

value.notes: Defensive posture consumes scarce European capacity.

value.title: Coordinated Sanctions and Oil Reserve Release
value.description: Tighten sanctions on Iranian military and petrochemical networks while releasing strategic reserves to contain energy shock.

response.cost_rule: PRC/RU may blunt sanctions through gray-market flows.

value.notes: Economic coercion also strains European consumers.

value.title: Humanitarian Corridor and Legal Review
value.description: Condition Blue support on humanitarian access and legal review while funding relief for displaced civilians.

response.cost_rule: Israel may accept constraints or lose coalition cohesion.

value.notes: Improves legitimacy but may slow Blue operations.

value.title: April Ceasefire Mediation Package
value.description: Broker temporary ceasefire with humanitarian relief, Hormuz stand-down, and monitored missile pause.

response.cost_rule: All major belligerents must accept or modify terms.

value.notes: Most effective if PRC or US ceasefire cards also played.

value.title: Damascus Consulate Strike
value.description: Precision strike kills senior IRGC-Quds Force personnel in Damascus and disrupts Levant logistics.

response.cost_rule: Iran gains access to direct retaliation cards.

value.notes: Tactical success changes rules of engagement.

private_conditions.description: Israel has preserved access to Hezbollah/IRGC communications networks.

value.title: Decapitation of Hezbollah / Northern Arrows
value.description: Exploit intelligence penetration to shatter Hezbollah communications and leadership, followed by border-clearing operations.

response.cost_rule: Iran may activate remaining proxies; NATO/EU may impose humanitarian constraints.

outcome_map.label: Hezbollah command shock
outcome_map.narrative: Hezbollah is badly degraded; Iran seeks alternative deterrence.

outcome_map.label: Partial decapitation
outcome_map.narrative: Leadership losses but remnants continue fire.

outcome_map.label: Hezbollah disperses
outcome_map.narrative: Operation becomes a longer Lebanon war.

value.notes: Strategic gain creates nuclear incentive and humanitarian costs.

value.title: Operation Days of Repentance
value.description: Long-range standoff strikes destroy Iranian air defenses, missile-production nodes, and Parchin research assets.

prerequisites.description: Iran has launched direct ballistic/cruise missile attacks or escalation flag is 3+.

response.cost_rule: Iran may accelerate nuclear risk; Russia may provide technical repair support.

value.notes: Shapes battlespace for later strikes but corners Iran.

value.title: Roaring Lion Leadership Hunt
value.description: Coordinated strikes target Iranian senior military and political leadership during regime crisis.

prerequisites.description: US has signaled support or Epic Fury is active.

response.cost_rule: Iran may harden succession; PRC/RU may denounce regime-change strikes.

value.notes: May remove leaders without ending regime.

value.title: Golan and Northern Border Fortification
value.description: Rapidly reinforce Golan and northern Israel against Syrian spillover and renewed Hezbollah attacks.

response.cost_rule: Hezbollah may pin Israeli forces in South Lebanon.

value.notes: Secures frontier but reduces operational flexibility.

value.title: Operation True Promise
value.description: Mass drone, cruise missile, and ballistic missile retaliation from Iranian territory with proxy diversionary launches.

play_constraints.frequency: Once; Turn 1 or after Israeli strike on Iranian leadership/diplomatic facility.

prerequisites.description: Iranian direct retaliation authorized by Israeli escalation or White Cell.

response.cost_rule: Blue may play air-defense responses.

modifiers.description: If Integrated Air Defense Coalition active, subtract 3.

outcome_map.label: Intercepted salvo
outcome_map.narrative: Iran saves face but exposes weakness.

outcome_map.label: Major Israeli damage
outcome_map.narrative: Iran restores deterrence but invites severe retaliation.

value.notes: Face-saving strike that may benefit Blue defense coalition.

private_conditions.description: Iran debates weaponization as proxy deterrent collapses.

value.title: Accelerated Nuclear Deterrence Debate
value.description: Hardliners push breakout preparations as Hezbollah and Syrian routes collapse.

prerequisites.description: Iran Proxy Network has been reduced or Assad collapse flag active.

response.cost_rule: Blue may use nuclear-strike cards if risk threshold is crossed.

value.notes: Improves deterrent narrative but invites preemption.

value.title: Proxy Retaliation Network
value.description: Activate Hezbollah remnants, Iraqi-Syrian militias, and Houthi-linked disruption to stretch Blue defenses.

play_constraints.frequency: Once per game, or once more if Proxy Network remains 3+.

response.cost_rule: Blue may strike proxies, increasing humanitarian pressure.

value.mission: Rocket, drone, mortar, and ATGM attacks into northern Israel

value.notes: Useful disruption, but degraded proxies may fail or trigger devastation in Lebanon.

value.title: Closure of the Strait of Hormuz
value.description: Announce closure of Hormuz using mines, coastal missiles, fast boats, and selective vessel vetting.

play_constraints.frequency: Once; Turn 5 or after regime-change attacks.

prerequisites.description: Major US/Israeli strikes or regime survival crisis is active.

response.cost_rule: US may counter-blockade; PRC may demand exemptions.

value.notes: Iran’s strongest coercive lever; also damages Iran and partners.

private_conditions.description: IRGC command prioritizes elite cohesion and blackout enforcement.

value.title: Internet Blackout and IRGC Crackdown
value.description: Suppress mass protests with nationwide communications blackout and security-force mobilization.

play_constraints.frequency: Once; Turn 4 after protests or legitimacy at 1 or less.

prerequisites.description: Iranian protest domestic event occurred or Domestic Legitimacy <= 1.

response.cost_rule: Opponents may exploit repression narrative.

value.notes: May stabilize regime short term while validating regime-change narratives.

value.title: Energy Exemption Bargain
value.description: Negotiate Chinese and nonaligned vessel exemptions through Hormuz while calling for de-escalation.

response.cost_rule: US may accuse PRC of undermining pressure; Iran may demand payment.

value.notes: Stabilizes PRC energy but divides pressure campaign.

value.title: UN Ceasefire Resolution and Legal Offensive
value.description: Mobilize Security Council diplomacy and global media on civilian harm, legality, and ceasefire terms.

play_constraints.frequency: Once; any turn after major Blue strike.

prerequisites.description: US or Israel has conducted major strike inside Iran or Lebanon.

response.cost_rule: Blue may concede corridors or absorb legal pressure.

value.notes: Constrains Blue politically while appearing stabilizing.

private_conditions.description: Commercial networks can move sanctioned components without overt military transfer.

value.title: Sanctions-Evasion and Dual-Use Supply Chain
value.description: Provide gray-market electronics, refinery support, and missile-industrial components through cutouts.

response.cost_rule: Blue may impose sanctions or expose network.

value.notes: Helps Iran rebuild but risks exposure.

value.title: Beijing Stabilization Conference
value.description: Host emergency talks linking Hormuz reopening, sanctions relief, and Iranian regime survival guarantees.

response.cost_rule: Acceptance may require concessions from all sides.

value.notes: May end fighting on terms less favorable to Blue.

value.title: Security Council Obstruction
value.description: Use veto threats and procedural delay to shield Iran and complicate Western legal narratives.

response.cost_rule: PRC may coordinate or distance itself.

value.notes: Cheap diplomatic friction.

private_conditions.description: Russian technicians can advise Iranian air-defense repair but are constrained by Ukraine war.

value.title: Air-Defense Repair and Radar Advisors
value.description: Provide limited S-300/radar repair assistance and tactical adaptation advice to Iran.

prerequisites.description: Iranian air defenses have been degraded.

response.cost_rule: Blue may target repair nodes or expose Russian role.

value.notes: Limited help; risks direct confrontation.

value.title: Energy Market Windfall and Sanctions Bargain
value.description: Exploit oil-price spike and offer replacement energy flows in exchange for quiet sanctions flexibility.

play_constraints.frequency: Once; after Hormuz instability or energy shock.

prerequisites.description: Maritime/Energy Stability scenario flag is 1 or lower, or Hormuz closed.

response.cost_rule: Europe may accept market relief at political cost.

value.notes: Market relief empowers Moscow.

value.title: Syrian Vacuum Leverage
value.description: Use residual networks in Syria to shape post-Assad power brokerage and preserve access where possible.

response.cost_rule: Israel may fortify Golan or strike logistics.

value.notes: Preserves influence but may destabilize Israel’s border.

value.title: 2026 Iranian Protests and Internet Blackout Trigger
value.description: Mass protests erupt amid economic collapse, military defeats, infrastructure failure, and elite factional rifts.

response.cost_rule: Iran may play crackdown; Blue may exploit legitimacy crisis.

value.notes: Domestic crisis opens regime-change opportunities but may lead to repression.

international_event.title: Turn 1: Damascus Strike and Operation True Promise
international_event.description: The rules of engagement shift from proxy conflict to direct Israel-Iran state-on-state exchange.

international_event.title: Turn 2: Proxy Collapse and Assad Regime Fall
international_event.description: Hezbollah decapitation and Syrian regime collapse sever Iran’s Levant land bridge.
international_event.notes: Makes Iranian nuclear and maritime options more attractive.

international_event.title: Turn 3: Twelve-Day War Threshold
international_event.description: If nuclear risk and escalation are high, Israeli and US strikes may trigger large-scale missile exchanges.
international_event.notes: White Cell should escalate if Nuclear Risk >=4 and no off-ramp exists.

international_event.title: Turn 5: Hormuz, Lebanon, and Ceasefire Crisis
international_event.description: Maritime blockade, renewed Hezbollah fighting, energy shock, and humanitarian exhaustion force ceasefire bargaining.
international_event.notes: Endgame focuses on bargaining, not clean military victory.

table_extensions.value: Measures usable force advantage and survivability.
Measures regime or government political resilience.
Measures unity, interoperability, and political tolerance of partners.
At 4+, Blue may justify preemptive deep strikes; at 6, uncontrolled nuclear crisis.
Measures Iran-aligned proxy capacity and reliability.
At 1 or lower, global energy crisis; at 0, severe economic shock.
At 5+, Blue coalition suffers automatic cohesion checks.
At 6+, White Cell may trigger theater-wide war complications.

marker_chits.label: Hormuz Closed
marker_chits.description: Commercial traffic is severely disrupted; energy stability falls.

marker_chits.label: Temporary Ceasefire
marker_chits.description: Kinetic operations paused subject to violations and bargaining.

marker_chits.label: Iranian Succession Crisis
marker_chits.description: Leadership decapitation or succession emergency affects regime cohesion.

marker_chits.label: Assad Collapse
marker_chits.description: Iranian land bridge to the Levant is severed.

marker_chits.label: Legal/Humanitarian Backlash
marker_chits.description: Coalition support strained by civilian harm and legality disputes.

marker_chits.label: Nuclear Threshold
marker_chits.description: Iranian nuclear risk has crossed crisis threshold.
